Title: Phloem-specific translational regulation of soybean nodulation: Insights from a phloem-targeted TRAP-seq approach

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Phloem-specific overexpression of the PME domain increased nodule number and reduced callose deposition, whereas overexpression of the PMEI domain decreased nodule number and increased callose deposition; RNAi had no significant effect likely due to redundancy.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Glyma.01G040700 promoter (phloem-specific); GmPME25 promoter for reporter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter::Ds-RED assay; phloem-specific domain overexpression (PME vs PMEI); RNAi knockdown; aniline blue callose staining; nodule counting; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi showed no obvious phenotype, suggesting redundancy among PME family members; altered plasmodesmata permeability/callose may have broader transport effects not fully tested.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein contains N-terminal PMEI domain and C-terminal PME domain plus signal peptide/transmembrane region; study emphasizes role in plasmodesmata permeability and symplastic connectivity rather than direct nutrient biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell wall/secretory pathway (strongly inferred from PME/PMEI domain structure and function)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports symplastic signaling and transport required for efficient nodulation and nitrogen-use coordination.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmPME25 is activated in root phloem during early nodulation. 2. Increasing PME activity promotes nodulation and reduces callose, consistent with increased plasmodesmata permeability. 3. Increasing PMEI activity has the opposite effect, indicating PME/PMEI balance regulates symplastic connectivity during nodulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in root phloem at 72 hpi during rhizobial infection; phloem-associated expression also discussed during nodulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root phloem during early nodulation (72 hpi), with broader PME/PMEI regulation through 21 dpi.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pectin methylesterase with intrinsic PMEI domain; signal peptide/transmembrane region
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Phloem-targeted tuning of PME/PMEI balance may improve nodulation efficiency and nitrogen-use efficiency, but redundancy and possible transport side effects should be considered.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Phloem-enriched GmAAP2 is upregulated at 72 hpi, suggesting a role in early amino acid/nitrogen supply to support nodule formation; expression declines by 21 dpi.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct functional validation in this paper; inferred function only.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as major transporter for xylem-to-phloem amino acid transfer during early nodulation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen supply for early nodule growth and development.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmAAP2 transcripts are enriched in phloem during early nodulation. 2. Expression pattern suggests a role in early nitrogen/amino acid supply. 3. Expression declines as nodules mature, consistent with reduced dependence on external amino acid input.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly upregulated in phloem at 72 hpi; declines at 21 dpi.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root phloem during nodulation, especially early stage.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to optimize early nitrogen supply during nodulation, though direct validation is needed.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GmAAP8 expression is unchanged at 72 hpi but significantly increased at 21 dpi, suggesting a role in later redistribution of fixed nitrogen products from nodules.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct functional validation in this paper; inferred function only.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interpreted as a regulator of later-stage source-to-sink nitrogen partitioning.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Redistribution of fixed nitrogen products to shoots/seeds and improving nitrogen-use efficiency.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmAAP8 is induced in phloem at late nodulation stage. 2. Expression pattern suggests involvement in partitioning glutamine, asparagine, and ureides after nitrogen fixation becomes active. 3. It likely complements early-stage GmAAP2 function by acting later in nodule maturation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Unchanged at 72 hpi, increased at 21 dpi in phloem.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Phloem transport stream to sink tissues
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving fixed-nitrogen redistribution and whole-plant nitrogen-use efficiency, pending direct testing.

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmbHLH121 is a phloem-induced bHLH transcription factor during rhizobial infection. 2. RNAi knockdown increases nodule number, indicating GmbHLH121 is a negative regulator of nodulation. 3. Overexpression does not significantly alter nodulation, and early nodulation marker genes are unchanged, suggesting action downstream of or independently from early nodulation signaling markers.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Enriched in phloem at 72 hpi and 21 dpi; promoter induced in phloem upon rhizobial infection.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens inoculation; nodulation stages 72 hpi and 21 dpi
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports control of nodulation and thereby biological nitrogen acquisition.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of nodule formation in soybean roots.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key phloem-specific negative regulator of nodule number during nodulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely transcriptional regulation; exact direct targets not established.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RNAi construct likely co-silenced close homolog GmbHLH122 but not GmbHLH123; histology showed nodule structure unchanged, indicating primary effect on nodule number not morphology; ENOD40, NIN, and NSP were unchanged.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused no significant nodulation phenotype; possible redundancy/co-silencing with homologs complicates interpretation.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GmbHLH121 promoter for GUS; overexpression promoter in pCAM-Ruby-Ox not clearly specified; RNAi construct in pCAMRUBY-CvMV-GWi
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: No significant effect detected on ENOD40; NIN; NSP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter::GUS assay; RNAi knockdown; overexpression in hairy roots; RT-qPCR; nodule counting; histology
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nodulation regulation linked to symbiotic nitrogen acquisition
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in root phloem and nodulation-associated vascular tissues after inoculation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor, Myc-like, subclade IVa
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation/editing of GmbHLH121 may increase nodule number and potentially enhance biological nitrogen input, but redundancy and effects on symbiotic balance should be evaluated.
